title: Structural Systems
editorialSummary: Building structural systems grouped by the dominant load-resisting mechanism. False trail: a Suspension Bridge is primarily a tension structure but its towers work in compression — the puzzle classifies it under Tension Structures because the cables (not the towers) define the primary structural logic. Geodesic Dome appears under Shell Structures because it distributes load through its surface geometry, not through compression in the traditional arch sense.

meta.gptRationale: Structural-engineering categories are specialized, and terms like tensegrity, monocoque, and folded plate push it toward the harder end of the tier.
